body.title: Repo auditor: audit_flags DB table in Hubert
body.result: Validated: hubert.audit_flags table live in DB (cecf1fb8). INSERT round-trip verified. Migration idempotent (re-run clean). 10-column schema matches spec.
body.intention: I intend that a durable audit_flags table exists in the DB so the auditor can write structured flags that persist across runs and are queryable by the web UI.
body.description:
Create the `hubert.audit_flags` table to store per-ticket-per-commit structural audit results.

Schema: `(id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, ticket_id TEXT, commit_hash TEXT, size TEXT, structural_score REAL, diff_lines INT, flag_reason TEXT, reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, verdict TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now())`.

Also create a migration file under `unseen_university/migrations/`.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/migrations/m_hubert_audit_flags.py (new), devices/hubert/__init__.py (schema registration)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; postgres-only
**Scope boundary:** IN — table creation + migration. OUT — populating data, web surface, cron wiring.
**Completion criteria:** `psql \dt hubert.*` includes `audit_flags`; INSERT of a sample row succeeds; migration script is idempotent (re-running produces no error).

## Escalation summary
**What was tried:** MAX_TURNS: hit 20 turns without DONE: prefix
**Where it broke:** max turns hit without completing
**What now?**
